name: Artificial intelligence
blurb: Machines that simulate human intelligence to reason, learn, and solve problems.

name: Hard disk drive
blurb: Stores data on spinning magnetic platters for persistent computer storage.

name: Nuclear power
blurb: Generates electricity from heat produced by controlled nuclear fission chain reactions in a reactor.

name: Biotechnology
blurb: Uses living systems and organisms to develop products, medicines, and industrial processes.

name: Nanotechnology
blurb: Engineers functional devices and materials at the scale of individual atoms and molecules.

name: Cryptocurrency
blurb: Decentralized digital currency secured by cryptographic proof instead of a central bank.

name: Genetic engineering
blurb: Directly modifies an organism's DNA to change its traits or function.

name: Blockchain
blurb: Distributes transaction records across a tamper-resistant chain of cryptographic blocks.

name: Virtual reality
blurb: Immerses the user in a fully computer-generated 3D environment via a headset.

name: Machine learning
blurb: Algorithms that learn patterns from data to make predictions or decisions without explicit programming.

name: Magnetic resonance imaging
blurb: Uses magnetic fields and radio waves to image soft tissue inside the body.

name: Robotics
blurb: Designs, builds, and operates machines that carry out tasks autonomously or semi-autonomously.

name: Machine translation
blurb: Automatically translates text or speech from one human language to another.

name: Cloning
blurb: Creates a genetically identical copy of an organism from a single cell's DNA.

name: Liquid-crystal display
blurb: Display technology using liquid crystals modulated by electric fields to produce images.

name: CT scan
blurb: Produces detailed cross-sectional images of the body by combining many X-ray measurements.

name: Fax
blurb: Transmits scanned documents over telephone lines to a remote printer.

name: Holography
blurb: Records and reconstructs 3-D light-field images using laser interference patterns.

name: Neural network (machine learning)
blurb: Layered computational model loosely inspired by neurons that learns patterns from data.

name: Natural language processing
blurb: Enables computers to understand, interpret, and generate human language.

name: Automation
blurb: Uses machines and software to perform tasks with minimal or no human intervention.

name: Graphene
blurb: Single-atom-thick carbon layer that is stronger than steel and highly conductive.

name: Biometrics
blurb: Identifies or verifies individuals using unique physical traits like fingerprints or irises.

name: Endoscopy
blurb: Inserts a camera through a small incision to inspect or treat internal organs.

name: Superfluidity
blurb: A fluid flows with zero viscosity, experiencing no friction or energy loss.

name: Electroencephalography
blurb: Measures the brain's electrical activity via electrodes placed on the scalp.

name: Augmented reality
blurb: Overlays digital information onto the real world via displays or smartphones.

name: Radio-frequency identification
blurb: Uses radio waves to wirelessly read data stored on small embedded tags.

name: Quantum computer
blurb: Uses quantum bits to solve problems exponentially faster than classical computers.

name: Fullerene
blurb: Hollow cage-like carbon molecules with unique chemical and material properties.

name: Aerogel
blurb: Ultralight solid material made of air-filled nanopores, one of the best insulators known.

name: Prosthesis
blurb: An artificial limb or device that replaces or augments a missing body part.

name: Teleportation
blurb: Transfers matter or information from one location to another without crossing the space between.

name: Self-driving car
blurb: A vehicle that navigates and drives itself using sensors, cameras, and AI.

name: Space elevator
blurb: A tether from Earth's surface to orbit lets payloads be lifted without rockets.

name: Dyson sphere
blurb: A megastructure enclosing a star to capture virtually all of its energy output.

name: Terraforming
blurb: Transforms a planet's atmosphere, temperature, and surface to support Earth life.

name: Cryonics
blurb: Preserves human bodies at ultra-low temperatures after death in hopes of future revival.

name: Artificial general intelligence
blurb: An AI that matches or exceeds human cognitive ability across all domains.

name: Fusion power
blurb: Generates electricity by fusing light atomic nuclei, mimicking the sun.

name: Humanoid robot
blurb: A robot with a body shaped and proportioned like a human.

name: Cold fusion
blurb: Hypothetical nuclear fusion achieved near room temperature rather than at stellar temperatures.

name: CRISPR gene editing
blurb: Precisely cuts and rewrites DNA sequences using Cas9 protein guided by RNA.

name: Reversible computing
blurb: Computation engineered to preserve all inputs, enabling reversal and theoretically near-zero-energy operation.
